channel.title: Consummate Athlete Podcast
channel.itunes:summary: Tune in weekly for expert insights on endurance training, cross-training, and performance, tailored specifically for masters-aged cyclists and runners. Hosted by coach Peter Glassford and author Molly Hurford, who each bring 20+ years of experience to help you enhance your performance and overall health.

item.title: Higher Function Through Pelvic Physiotherapy - Laura Powers Returns
item.itunes:title: Higher Function Through Pelvic Physiotherapy - Laura Powers Returns
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly interviews returning guest and Physiotherapist Laura Powers:

  • What is Pelvic Health and Pelvic Physiotherapy
  • How Pelvic Health relates to different stages of life 
  • Pelvic health is not just for women or post-pregnancy 
  • What is the Pelvic Floor
  • Signs and symptoms that indicate seeing a pelvic physiotherapist 

💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

 

This Episode is Brought to You By:

item.title: The Difference Between A Leadville Sub 12 vs. Sub 9 Hour Finish?
item.itunes:title: The Difference Between A Leadville Sub 12 vs. Sub 9 Hour Finish?
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer a listener question about:

  • THE Leadville 8 Month Training Plan available now on Training Peaks 
  • The difference in workout/plan for those who finish in sub-9 hour and sub-12 hour
  • Genetics, Lifestyle, sport history, age and other factors that influence finish time
  • Tweaks and expectations in a training plan for faster finishes

💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

item.title: Late in Day Hunger + Heart Rate Decoupling
item.itunes:title: Late in Day Hunger + Heart Rate Decoupling
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer listener questions about : 

  • Late in the day energy crashes 
  • Timing of fueling and eating before workouts
  • the concept of aerobic decoupling and Heart Rate Drift 
  • Creatine Supplements

item.title: Physical Activity Variety and Risk of Death
item.itunes:title: Physical Activity Variety and Risk of Death
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly discuss a recent study performed by researchers at Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health that associates the variety of movement done with a reduced risk of premature death, even at lower volumes of exercise. 

  • Implications for athletes and for play!
  • How varied strength training might help
  • The 4'cs for Consummate Athlete
  • Adding more variety to indoor trainer workouts 
  • Thinking beyond organized sport to movements 

💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

 

This Episode is Brought to You By:

 

🛠️ Try the Payable Add-On for your next event, project or order-form to get paid in a google form:  PayableApps.com/ATHLETE – No app fees for 6 months!

item.title: Indoor Cycling Boredom, ERG and FTP Setting
item.itunes:title: Indoor Cycling Boredom, ERG and FTP Setting
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly discuss:

  • ERG mode for smart trainers and where it can do you wrong
  • adding variety and engagement to indoor cycling
  • Setting threshold for indoor cycling training
  • indoor cycling mistakes

item.title: Don't Accept Numbness - BikeFit - Tim Plunkett
item.itunes:title: Don't Accept Numbness - BikeFit - Tim Plunkett
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter interviews Tim Plunkett from SpeedRiver Bicycles in Guelph, Ontario about:

  • Strategies for reducing saddle numbness or discomfort
  • best practices in choosing saddles
  • New tech in saddles and shoes
  • The increase in numbness that riders are dealing with
  • Indoor Riding has increase the need for bikefit 
  • Youth are riding indoors more 
  •

item.title: Dr. Scott Frey Returns - Mental Toughness + Goal Setting
item.itunes:title: Dr. Scott Frey Returns - Mental Toughness + Goal Setting
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly Hurford interviews Dr. Scott Frey, who returns to the show for a 2nd time! This interview discusses: 

  • Mental Toughness

item.title: All Bodies on Bikes - Marley Blonsky
item.itunes:title: All Bodies on Bikes - Marley Blonsky
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly talks to All Bodies on Bikes Co-Founder Marley Blonsky. 

In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly sat down with All Bodies on Bikes co-founder Marley Blonsky in Bentonville around Big Sugar to explore how cycling can become truly inclusive for riders of all sizes. They discuss building body-size-inclusive communities, making movement joyful and sustainable, bike camping as an accessible adventure, and the importance of clear, respectful language around body size.

Marley's new book: https://stronggirlpublishing.com/ragtag

item.description:

item.itunes:title: HRV, Heart Rate and Fatigue - Should I train?
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer listener questions on:
  • HRV baseline and how to take action on resting heart rate and hrv
  • Using feeling and muscle soreness as primary and HRV to support decisions 
  • Considering fueling and training load when finding disruption 
  • Seasonal undulations in HRV and 'life stress' with school, new sports etc.

item.title: How to Train After Your Goal Race or with No Upcoming Goal
item.itunes:title: How to Train After Your Goal Race or with No Upcoming Goal
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer a listener questions about how to train after you recover from your big race and when you don't have an upcoming race to train for.  We discuss: 
  • Returning to early base or 'general preparation' phases 
  • Revisiting gaps and limiters so you can improve your performance in future events
  • Goal setting process we use with the athletes we coach
  • Easing into cross-training and strength to prepare for upcoming heavier phases. 
💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

item.title: How Long Does It Take To Recover from Big Adventures?
item.itunes:title: How Long Does It Take To Recover from Big Adventures?
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer a listener question about how long it takes to recover from a multi-day endurance event. Topics include:
  • Factors that affect time till recovery after events
  • Types of recovery to consider
  • Risks of under-recovery 
  • Variations in recovery with event type, duration and intensity

item.title: Menopause for Active Women - Selene Yeager
item.itunes:title: Menopause for Active Women - Selene Yeager
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly interview Selene Yeager of Feisty Menopause and the 'Hit Play Not Pause' podcast. 
 
Topics include: 
  • Updates on reasearch and best practices for Menopause
  • Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT) updates and misconceptions 
  • Ways a coach can serve those experiencing menopause symptoms 
  • Training adjustments for menopause
  • Mindset and Identity through Menopause transition 
  • Fueling mistakes for menopausal women

  • MTB Workouts - how to train on mtb if its rainy or not have access to trail? 
  • How to get better at climbing
  • Why isn't your actual TSS matching prescribed TSS?

  • How and R.D. can help with your fueling for endurance sports
  • Common fueling errors for gravel cyclists 
  • The challenge of weight loss and training - can you do both? 
  • Creatine, Baking Soda and more !

item.title: Molly Wins Sulphur Springs Trail Race 2025!
item.itunes:title: Molly Wins Sulphur Springs Trail Race 2025!
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter interviews Molly about her win at the Sulphur Springs Trail race in 2025. The episode includes discussions about: 
  • What is the Sulphur Springs Trail Race + event highlights
  • How the Race was Won: Sulphur Springs 100 Miler 2025 (17:22 win time)
  • Using a Hydration pack in running races
  • Crewing & Pacing Tips
  • Feed / aid station strategies for short stops 
  • Pacing 100 milers 
  • Tapering and Training into the 100 
  • Low moments (like vomitting and pre-race nerves) & getting past them!

item.title: Can you Train Off-Road for Cycling? + Can you Stop in Your Training Rides?
item.itunes:title: Can you Train Off-Road for Cycling? + Can you Stop in Your Training Rides?
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer listener questions about making stops during training rides and a question about whether it is possible to train off-road for cycling (rather than on the indoor trainer): 

  • We discuss what training is and concepts like specificity 
  • We discuss the importance of the environment and determinants of race day performance
  • stopping in training pros and cons and how to fit things like coffee stops into rides
  • How long of a stop is too long? or when a ride becomes two rides. 

💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

item.title: Strength Training for Endurance Performance - Dan Cleather
item.itunes:title: Strength Training for Endurance Performance - Dan Cleather
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter talks with Dan Cleather who returns for the second time on the podcast. Dan is a Professor of Strength and Conditioning at St Mary's University and has coached World and Olympic champions in athletics, rowing, canoeing and rugby. 
 
Today we cover: 
  • What strength could/should look like for endurance athletes
  • Common mistakes and reasons endurance athletes stop strength training
  • The importance of thinking about force expression, not just metabolism and access to energy. 
  • Practical on-bike workouts to improve performance
 
💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

item.title: Ideas for Your Final Race Prep Workout + Riding and Racing Unfamiliar Trails
item.itunes:title: Ideas for Your Final Race Prep Workout + Riding and Racing Unfamiliar Trails
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter and Molly answer listener questions on:
  • Best strategies for those final big 'race simualtion workouts' before big endurance races. When to do these final workouts that we call 'GAMEPLAY' workouts. What they might look like ideally and how to interpret the results whether the test ride goes great or leaves room for improvement . 
  • How to better ride and race on trails that you haven't seen (racing 'blind' ) 
💬 Have a question for a Future Episode? Submit it anonymously here

item.title: Transitioning Training Outside + Can You Do Too Much Cross-Training?
item.itunes:title: Transitioning Training Outside + Can You Do Too Much Cross-Training?
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly Hurford and Peter Glassford discuss 4 listener questions including Hardtail vs. Dual-Suspension, Packs vs. Vests Vs. Bottles Vs. Hip Packs, and then using Structured workouts outside (Spring transition for cycling) and finally, how to know if you are cross-training too much! (#CrosstrainingIsNotACrime)

item.title: Should I Do 3 Hours on the Trainer ?
item.itunes:title: Should I Do 3 Hours on the Trainer ?
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly and Peter discuss a few listener questions. Firstly a question about when, why and how to ride 3 hours (or more) on the trainer. We also discuss logging TSS for strength (and why you might not want to) and why/where/how to do testing to assess your fitness.

item.title: Is it Okay to Train to Be Competitive on Group Rides?
item.itunes:title: Is it Okay to Train to Be Competitive on Group Rides?
item.description:
In this episode Peter and Molly discuss whether you can train with a coach or follow a training plan simply to get fitter or to do great on a group ride or big adventure with friends. We discuss how different athletes pursue different goals that can look like FKTs, cycling tours, weekly races and more!

item.title: Race Priority - Selecting the Right Mix of Events
item.itunes:title: Race Priority - Selecting the Right Mix of Events
item.description:
In this episode of The Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly and Peter discuss a listener question about prioritizing races using the A, B, C method. They break down what each category means and how it impacts training for masters cyclists and runners. Plus, they explore the often-overlooked factors of challenge, pressure, and inconvenience—ensuring you strike the right balance in your events between pushing your limits and avoiding burnout. Refine your race strategy and set yourself up for success!

item.title: Beyond Survival Mode: Unlock Your Potential - Steve Magness
item.itunes:title: Beyond Survival Mode: Unlock Your Potential - Steve Magness
item.description:
This episode of The Consummate Athlete, we sit down with performance expert and bestselling author, Steve Magness, to unpack the core principles of his groundbreaking new book, Win the Inside Game. Steve shares his personal journey from the high-pressure world of professional coaching, including his experiences as a whistleblower, to reveal why so many of us struggle to thrive in the face of external pressure. Steve shares his framework for success and numerous practical insights and tactics to action in your training this week.
 
PRIZE - One BOOK Give-a-way - Deadline is Feb 14th 2025 at midnight E.S.T. for submission by sending us a picture of a rating/review of the podcast or by posting a story of the episode on Instagram. Contest limited to Continental USA and Canada.

This episode of The Consummate Athlete, we sit down with performance expert and bestselling author, Steve Magness, to unpack the core principles of his groundbreaking new book, Win the Inside Game. Steve shares his personal journey from the high-pressure world of professional coaching, including his experiences as a whistleblower, to reveal why so many of us struggle to thrive in the face of external pressure. Steve shares his framework for success and numerous practical insights and tactics to action in your training this week. PRIZE - One BOOK Give-a-way - Deadline is Feb 14th 2025 at midnight E.S.T. for submission by sending us a picture of a rating/review of the podcast or by posting a story of the episode on Instagram. item.title: Can You Maintain Or Improve Your Cycling Performance As You Age?
item.itunes:title: Can You Maintain Or Improve Your Cycling Performance As You Age?
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we answer listener questions about whether we can still make progress as we age. Can we perform as well or better? and what is the difference between health, fitness and performance? We also discuss what it means if you can't finish a workout and whether to reduce your threshold setting for cycling workouts.

item.title: Winning at UnboundXL and Ultra Gravel Distances - Kristen Legan
item.itunes:title: Winning at UnboundXL and Ultra Gravel Distances - Kristen Legan
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we chat with 2023 Unbound XL winner and gravel coach Kristen Legan. She answers listener questions about training for ultra-long gravel events, covering topics like fueling, rest stops, training hours, and key workouts. Grab your notepad—this is an info-packed interview you won't want to miss!

item.title: How to, Why to, When to Set Max Heart Rate
item.itunes:title: How to, Why to, When to Set Max Heart Rate
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we answer a listener question about setting your max heart rate so that you can train by zones set by maximal heart rate. We also discuss variations on setting zones for those who do not have past data from hard efforts or who might not want to do maximal efforts. We Also discuss an analogy for new and sometimes random metrics that are on devices.

item.title: Common Cycling Metrics For the New Year
item.itunes:title: Common Cycling Metrics For the New Year
item.description:
Many Cyclists and Runners got new cycling gadgets over the holidays. You might have purchased or been gifted a new device or software to kick of your training for the cycling season. With these new devices come a host of metrics that can be useful (or not). Today we go through a few of the common cycling metrics and try to simplify them. This episode gives you some direction on what might be of use and reminders to follow your feeling and to have fun! Follow up questions are welcomed!

item.title: Your Diet Sucks with Kylee Van Horn and Zoë Rom
item.itunes:title: Your Diet Sucks with Kylee Van Horn and Zoë Rom
item.description:
Kylee Van Horn is a Registered Dietitian specializing in sports nutrition and Zoë Rom is a journalist and together they created a podcast called 'Your Diet Sucks' looking at diet culture and also what we can do to improve our relationship to food (or 'how to unf*ck your relationship to food', as they put it!)

In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Peter Glassford and Molly Hurford discuss a listener questiona about what Zone 2 is for a masters aged cyclist who wants to keep it simple.  The other question discussed is about the Festive 500 challenge. We discuss the origins of the challenge, some of the variations, and when it makes sense for an adult cyclist over the holidays.

item.title: Cold Weather Training and Performance Strategies for Cyclists - Dr. Stephen Cheung
item.itunes:title: Cold Weather Training and Performance Strategies for Cyclists - Dr. Stephen Cheung
item.description:
Dr. Cheung returns to the podcast to dive into the science of training and racing in cold weather for cycling and running. He breaks down the key differences between heat and cold adaptation, sharing insights into how the body responds and what that means for your training and performance. We also discuss trending interventions like cold plunges—can they actually improve performance in cold conditions, or are they more hype than help? Tune in to find out what works, what doesn't, and how to stay strong in the cold!

This COLD Episode is Brought to You By:

item.title: Season Review & Goal Setting for Adult Athletes
item.itunes:title: Season Review & Goal Setting for Adult Athletes
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we explore practical approaches to goal setting and share a framework you can use, whether you have a specific goal in mind or are looking for direction. We discuss how setting short-term process goals can help you progress toward larger annual goals, like achieving a fitness milestone or preparing for an event. Additionally, we highlight the importance of considering your other roles and life goals to create a season plan that is realistic, balanced, and effective.

This Episode is Brought to You By:

item.title: Life Skills Through Bike Skills - Gabrielle Rossi
item.itunes:title: Life Skills Through Bike Skills - Gabrielle Rossi
item.description:
In this episode, we chat with Gabrielle Rossi, PhD, the Executive Director of Girls in Gear, a confidence-building initiative for young cyclists that develops "life skills through bike skills." We explore the empowerment of youth through cycling, the cultivation of resilience, and Gabrielle's passion for fostering connections through biking. She shares how bicycles can inspire personal growth, from local initiatives to broader national programs. Tune in for an engaging conversation about leadership, cycling, and community impact!

This Episode is Brought to You By:

item.itunes:title: Sodium-Bicarbonate, Fall Weight Loss, Red-S - Stevie Lyn Smith
item.description:
This episode features Stevie Lyn Smith  a registered dietitian with a speciality in sports dietetics. Stevie speaks with Molly about a host of nutritional concepts that concern masters athletes including concerns about over-fueling, the risks of under-fueling, Red-S, Sodium-Bicarbonate, and the concept of fall weight loss when training load is lower.

item.title: Cold Weather Training, Heart Rate and Strategies for Success
item.itunes:title: Cold Weather Training, Heart Rate and Strategies for Success
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we answer listener questions on training through the chilly fall and early winter season, including a common question: why is heart rate often lower when it's cold out? We explore how to get the most out of heart rate-based training by considering your specific conditions and context. We also look at alternatives to training software like Zwift, such as watching bike races or movies for added motivation. Finally, we cover outdoor cold-weather riding tips, including dressing (and over-dressing) strategies to stay warm and comfortable.

item.title: Iceman Cometh 2024 - Noah Ramsey
item.itunes:title: Iceman Cometh 2024 - Noah Ramsey
item.description:
Noah Ramsey is the winner of the 2024 Bells Iceman Cometh Challenge and a U23 Mountain Bike Racer from Toronto, Ontario. We discuss his racing season, specifics about Iceman and where he might go next as he transitions from U23 to Elite in 2025. We also discuss his favorite workout that he has gotten from coach Peter over the last 5 years they have worked together.

item.title: Recovery, Physiology & Strength - Julia Casadio
item.itunes:title: Recovery, Physiology & Strength - Julia Casadio
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly Hurford chats with Julia Casadio, a high-performance sports expert who has worked with Olympians in canoe slalom and sailing. They dive into a range of topics, including Molly's ankle injury recovery, the benefits of wearables like the Oura Ring, and they consider heat training and cold plunging for athletes. Julia shares valuable insights on female athlete physiology, discussing the impact of menstrual cycles, hormonal IUDs, and strategies to maintain immunity during cold and flu season. They also touch on the critical role of sleep for athletic performance, and Julia introduces her upcoming "Her Strength Coach" course, designed to help coaches better support adolescent female athletes through physical and psychological changes.

 

This Episode is Brought to You By:

item.title: Can Group Rides be Part of a Trainig Plan + Including Heavy Strength Before MTB Race?
item.itunes:title: Can Group Rides be Part of a Trainig Plan + Including Heavy Strength Before MTB Race?
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we answer 2 listener questions about training plans. Can Group Rides be Part of a Training Plan and how do you include them around intervals and steady endurance riding without over or under loading? The second question is about including Heavy Strength Before MTB Race?   We go through how we think about general/goal-based plans that we put on training peaks and discuss the benefits of our 3 month Made for you Plan and coaching for those who are extra keen, use many modalities and who train year round.

item.title: Run Safety + Ontario Triple Crown of 100 milers - John Shep
item.itunes:title: Run Safety + Ontario Triple Crown of 100 milers - John Shep
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we chat with John Shep, a teacher, ultrarunner, and host of the Athletics Ontario Running Podcast, who recently accomplished the incredible feat of completing the Triple Crown of Ontario—three grueling 100-mile races: Sulfur Springs, Halliburton, and Tally in the Valley.

John discusses the importance of local racing, community support, and his favorite (and hardest) moments from the Triple Crown. John also opens up about a recent, terrifying experience of being hit by a car while out on a run, which leads us into an important conversation about runner safety. 

This Episode is Brought to You By:

item.title: Unlocking Off-Season Success: Transition Phases + Using Session RPE
item.itunes:title: Unlocking Off-Season Success: Transition Phases + Using Session RPE
item.description:
In this episode Molly and Peter discuss what a training plan or coaching might look like in the 'off-season', which is the periods of the year away from the competitive season. This phase is sometimes referred to as the transition or preparation phases and comes before the base or acccumulation phases. For athletes wanting to 'go to the next level' or improve their results this phase of the year might be the missing piece!  We also discuss Session RPE (sRPE) versus RPE and how to use feeling in your workouts. 

This Episode is Brought to You By:

The Payable Add-on for Google Forms – Try it out for your next Event, Pizza day, jersey order or clinic/camp!

This phase is sometimes referred to as the transition or preparation phases and comes before the base or acccumulation phases. For athletes wanting to 'go to the next level' or improve their results this phase of the year might be the missing piece! We also discuss Session RPE (sRPE) versus RPE and how to use feeling in your workouts. item.title: Rebecca Rusch on Concussions
item.itunes:title: Rebecca Rusch on Concussions
item.description:
Rebecca Rusch joins Peter and Molly for her third appearance on the Consummate Athlete Podcast to discuss her personal experience with a severe brain injury and her extended recovery. This journey inspired her to learn more about concussions and share her insights in a four-part podcast series with the Feisty Media's Women's Performance Podcast, titled Brain Storm.

item.title: Category Upgrades & Should You Keep Racing as You Get Older?
item.itunes:title: Category Upgrades & Should You Keep Racing as You Get Older?
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we tackle two important questions for masters cyclists about racing, category upgrades, and the evolution of athletic goals as we get older. Coach Peter Glassford and Author Molly Hurford dive into the nuances of choosing the right race category and balancing performance with life priorities.

First, we answer a listener's dilemma: Should you race in a category where you can build confidence and work on race craft, or should you jump into a tougher and/or longer race category? We discuss how to weigh factors like race duration, speed, and even how family and race scheduling might influence your decision.

Next, we explore the question of how to approach racing during life's transitions, especially as you get older. We discuss how several clients have balanced aging and racing. We also discuss how you can use 'events' and 'challenges' as well as a reduced race calendar to help keep cycling in your life the idea that you can certainly enter events with different goals and refine how many races you do in a season as well as what constitutes an event (e.g. bike packing or tours). 

This Episode is Brought to You By:

  • The Payable Add-on for Google Forms – Try it out for your next Event, Pizza day, jersey order or clinic/camp!

item.title: End of Season Adventures: How, Why, and Why Not to Go Big This Fall
item.itunes:title: End of Season Adventures: How, Why, and Why Not to Go Big This Fall
item.description:
Join Peter and Molly as they dive into the appeal of taking on last-minute, end-of-season endurance adventures—whether it's a big race, a challenging ride, or an epic endurance event. They share real-life examples from clients and explore how factors like sport, distance, and terrain, along with your current training, influence the risk of these spontaneous challenges. They also discuss the importance of understanding why you're drawn to the challenge and offer strategies for tackling similar goals with less risk by planning for something 4-8 weeks down the road that still satisfies your craving for adventure.

This Episode is Brought to You By:

item.title: Fall Fitness: How to Train When You're Between Races
item.itunes:title: Fall Fitness: How to Train When You're Between Races
item.description:
Join Peter and Molly as they discuss training strategies for the fall season—the period after your major "A" races when you may not have a specific goal on the horizon. They explore how your current motivation, injury, illness and life can influence the training you do. The episode highlights how, despite appearances, time before your next objective is limited due to upcoming winter conditions and the busy family and work lives many masters endurance athletes lead.

This Episode is brought to you by the Payable Add-on for Google Forms – Try it out for your next Event, Pizza day, jersey order or clinic/camp!

item.content:encoded:
Join Peter and Molly as they discuss training strategies for the fall season—the period after your major "A" races when you may not have a specific goal on the horizon. They explore how your current motivation, injury, illness and life can influence the training you do. The episode highlights how, despite appearances, time before your next objective is limited due to upcoming winter conditions and the busy family and work lives many masters endurance athletes lead.

item.title: Recovery between Races - Cyclocross, Masters-Athletes + Weeklys
item.itunes:title: Recovery between Races - Cyclocross, Masters-Athletes + Weeklys
item.description:
Peter and Molly discuss a common question in the late summer and early fall about recovering between intervals, races and weekly practices or practice cyclocross races. They look at the importance of planning the season, month and week to include recovery and also how masters athletes might adjust their week's further to optimize their training plan for their recovery needs.

item.title: All *Aboot* Leadville: The Race Recap Episode
item.itunes:title: All *Aboot* Leadville: The Race Recap Episode
item.description:
Molly Hurford recounts her experience at the Leaville 100 mile Run in 2024. We discuss actionable tips and strategies for preparing for ultra-endurance events, for events at altitude and specifically for Leadville events. Dealing with changing race goals, disapointment after an injury and whether to continue chasing goals after missing them.

item.title: How and When to Modify or End a Workout
item.itunes:title: How and When to Modify or End a Workout
item.description:
Join Peter and Molly as they tackle a common challenge faced by athletes: finding the time, energy, and focus to complete your workouts. A listener asks whether they should push through and stick to the plan or if they risk overtraining. Peter and Molly explore how to approach your training plan, offering strategies to modify workouts while staying on track with your goals.

item.title: What should your cadence be? + Including Sprints in Training
item.itunes:title: What should your cadence be? + Including Sprints in Training
item.description:

item.title: Is a High Heart Rate a Sign You Aren't Fit?
item.itunes:title: Is a High Heart Rate a Sign You Aren't Fit?
item.description:
In today's episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we dive into the concept of high heart rate during exercise. We explore various reasons behind a higher heart rate, including potential data errors and the influence of your training history. We'll also discuss whether it's valid to compare exercise heart rates between different people. Additionally, we examine the broader significance of heart rate monitoring and why it's a valuable tool for athletes. 

This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs

item.title: How to Review Your Race
item.itunes:title: How to Review Your Race
item.description:
In this week's episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly Hurford and Peter Glassford discuss analyzing race files and understanding the qualitative aspects of your performance. We discuss the balance of starting fast to get ahead without ending up fading later. Learn what different heart rate and power trends mean, why it's crucial not to judge your race while you're in it, and the importance of getting to the startline, and finishing as best you can as worthwhile goals, aside from any additional performance, race or stretch goals. Tune in for insights that will help you make the most of your race data and improve your overall racing experience.

This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. This ready to go plans are available to start whenever you are and provide a valuable and proven workouts to guide you to your goal or event. item.title: Road Marathon to Trail Ultra - Reid Coolsaet - Live Episode
item.itunes:title: Road Marathon to Trail Ultra - Reid Coolsaet - Live Episode
item.description:
Join us for a special live episode presented by Salomon at Vo2 Sports Co. in Collingwood, featuring Olympian, 2:10 marathoner, dad, coach, and ultra-marathon racer Reid Coolsaet. We discuss his transition to off-road racing, balancing Olympic-level training with family and work, and share specific training tips for uphills, downhills, and preventing ankle sprains.

item.title: How to Bring More Women to Trail Running - Live Episode
item.itunes:title: How to Bring More Women to Trail Running - Live Episode
item.description:
Join us for a special live episode presented by Salomon at Vo2 Sports Co. in Collingwood, Ontario. This panel discussion, "How to Bring More Women to Trail Running," features insights from four inspiring women: Erin Doucette, Sabrine Chanda, Christina Blackley, and moderator Molly Hurford. Discover strategies and stories to inspire more women to hit the trails, and delve into the complex factors that limit access to running for different people. Don't miss this empowering conversation!

 
This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. This ready to go plans are available to start whenever you are and provide a valuable and proven workouts to guide you to your goal or event. item.title: Race Recaps including Western States + Racing after Time Off + Dropper Posts + Trouble Doing Power Outside
item.itunes:title: Race Recaps including Western States + Racing after Time Off + Dropper Posts + Trouble Doing Power Outside
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly and Peter discuss several major events from the weekend, including the Tour de France, the Western States Running 100 Miler, and the Canadian Olympic Track Running Trials. We also discuss the Unchained Tour de France documentary and share key takeaways about mindset and self-talk, even for the best athletes in the world.

We address three listener questions, covering the challenges and strategies for racing after unplanned time off before an event, tips for learning to use your dropper seat posts, and tackling the common issue of struggling to maintain power outside compared to indoor training.

It's a packed episode full of insights, tips, and stories from the world of cycling and endurance sports!

 
This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. This ready to go plans are available to start whenever you are and provide a valuable and proven workouts to guide you to your goal or event. Use Code 'CAPOD' to get 25% of any of the plans . bit.ly/PGPLANS

 Find links to your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://pod.link/1100471297
 
Watch The Consummate Athlete Podcast on Youtube HERE

Show Notes

  • Perfect workout post - Consummate Athlete
  • 3 Legged LIon Story in New York TImes
  • Our popular post about TSS and 'how high should fitness score be' that discusses indoor vs. outdoor power
  • Last week's podcast on goals and expectations discussing the Roger Federer Quote 
  • The Roger Feder quote about losing half the points (e.g. expectation that will lose some)

item.title: Achieving Success with Realistic Event Expectations
item.itunes:title: Achieving Success with Realistic Event Expectations
item.description:
Today on the Consummate Athlete podcast, we discuss how our expectations can shape our success or failure. We cover the delicate balance of striving for progress and aspiring for more while avoiding unrealistic goals. Through athlete case studies and our personal experiences, we work through the nuances of the tricky art of goal setting.

item.title: Auto-Pausing Workouts - Implications for Data & Psychology
item.itunes:title: Auto-Pausing Workouts - Implications for Data & Psychology
item.description:
This week's podcast Q&A tackles the topic of AUTO-PAUSE on bike computers and run watches. We explore what auto-pausing is, why it might seem appealing, and the reasons you should consider not using it. We also read some responses to our recent post on the topic to help understand some of the questions and issues with auto-pause. You'll learn about the impact of auto-pausing on your workout data and the importance of recording elapsed time. Additionally, we touch on the psychology behind using auto-pause, what it could mean for your workouts, and how it might affect your long-term progress as a masters cyclist

item.title: Fueling, RED-S & Weight Management for Athletes with Lindsey Cortes RD
item.itunes:title: Fueling, RED-S & Weight Management for Athletes with Lindsey Cortes RD
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, Molly Hurford chats with sports dietitian Lindsey Cortes. We explore the staggering statistic that up to 45% of female athletes engage in some form of disordered eating. We discuss the reasons behind this, how it happens, and the signs and symptoms of Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport (RED-S). Lindsay shares insights on the biggest dietary pitfalls athletes face, not just during exercise but throughout the day. We also learn that RED-S can also affect athletes who aren't underweight, offering a broader perspective on this condition. 

 

This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs

item.title: Why am I Failing Workouts? + Should I Look at Heart Rate in a Race?
item.itunes:title: Why am I Failing Workouts? + Should I Look at Heart Rate in a Race?
item.description:
Today, Molly and Peter tackle two insightful listener questions on the Consummate Athlete podcast. First, they delve into the topic of failed workouts and how we might misinterpret success in endurance sports training. Then, they look at a second question about which metrics are worth monitoring (or ignoring) during a race to optimize performance. This episode will help you gain a deeper understanding of endurance training and racing strategies.

item.title: Do You Have to Do Your Race Distance in Training?
item.itunes:title: Do You Have to Do Your Race Distance in Training?
item.description:
Join Peter and Molly as they discuss the common question of how much you need to train to be ready for an endurance race. Do you need to complete the race distance or duration, or both! Or can you get by with shorter rides, that might allow more frequency or quality training? 

This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs

item.title: Heat Adaptation for Exercise Performance - Dr. Stephen Cheung Returns!
item.itunes:title: Heat Adaptation for Exercise Performance - Dr. Stephen Cheung Returns!
item.description:
Explore the latest insights on heat adaptation and exercise performance with expert Dr. Stephen Cheung. Discover how heat adaptation impacts human performance and how it can benefit endurance training for masters athletes. Gain valuable knowledge from a recent meta-analysis and practical strategies to optimize your training so that you can perform better in hot enviroments.

item.title: Can You Train for Cycling Outside?
item.itunes:title: Can You Train for Cycling Outside?
item.description:
This week, we discuss a common question Peter often receives, that might surprise you! Although cycling typically takes place outdoors, many athletes now start to train for cycling while indoors on smart trainers. Some even begin cycling indoors before considering taking their cycling outdoors for training and competition. This shift outdoors can lead to challenges when transitioning into a more variable enviroment. Join us as we explore how cyclists maintain structured training  for cycling outdoors while still enjoying the simple pleasure of riding a bike! 

This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs
 
This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. item.title: Can You Be Fit for 2 Different Types of Races?
item.itunes:title: Can You Be Fit for 2 Different Types of Races?
item.description:
In this episode, we discuss a listener's question about training for two different types of bike races, like Gravel and Mountain bike, within a single season. We explore how your pst race experience, goals for each event and current training status can influence your approach to this challenge. Additionally, we draw from our personal experiences balancing multiple disciplines simultaneously.

item.title: Down Bad, Crying at the Gym - Spring Blahs & Low Motivation
item.itunes:title: Down Bad, Crying at the Gym - Spring Blahs & Low Motivation
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we dive into the challenges many athletes face with low motivation, burnout, or what we like to call the 'spring blahs.' As you prepare for early races and navigate through varying weather conditions, including continued indoor riding and messy outdoor conditions, we explore mindset strategies, common errors in fueling, setting realistic expectations, effective goal setting, and essential preparation and equipment options to safeguard your performance. Tune in for valuable insights and practical tips to overcome these challenges and thrive in your athletic pursuits.

This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs
 
This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. item.title: An Athlete's Journey Through Knee Pain - Mackenzie Myatt
item.itunes:title: An Athlete's Journey Through Knee Pain - Mackenzie Myatt
item.description:
Join us for a special bonus episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, where Author, Poet and Elite Racer Mackenzie Myatt shares her recent experience with knee pain and some poetry about the experience! Check out her her recent book with Strong Girl Publishing, 'In Defense of Big Dreams'. Don't miss this inspiring conversation filled with valuable insights into resilience, perseverance, and the pursuit of big dreams in sport and life!

item.title: Training for Leadville 100 Run & Bike - 3 Months Out
item.itunes:title: Training for Leadville 100 Run & Bike - 3 Months Out
item.description:
Peter and Molly discuss preparing for the Leadville 100 run and bike. Molly is doing the run in 2024 and Peter has done the bike previously and coaches adult athletes to belt-buckle finishes each year! They discuss altitude prep, heat training. technical and tactical preparation as well as the value in trying your best to reduce other factors/projects ahead of A-Races.

item.title: Q2 Goals, Using a Whoop, Coaching if not a skilled rider & Late Workout Refueling

item.title: Year-Round Intensity + Starting Strength in 60's
item.itunes:title: Year-Round Intensity + Starting Strength in 60's
item.description:
Today Molly and Peter discuss a listener question about doing intensity and races year-round. They look at an interesting study about a person in their 60s starting to strength train and the benefits in muscle mass and strength they saw into their 70s! They also discuss a common question about what should 'count' as training and what to include in a training log.

Today Molly and Peter discuss a listener question about doing intensity and races year-round. They look at an interesting study about a person in their 60s starting to strength train and the benefits in muscle mass and strength they saw into their 70s! They also discuss a common question about what should 'count' as training and what to include in a training log.

item.title: Bike Packing & Indoor Training - Charlotte Backus
item.itunes:title: Bike Packing & Indoor Training - Charlotte Backus
item.description:
Join Molly on the Consummate Athlete Podcast as she interviews Charlotte Backus Turner, an Elite Cyclist and coach from Park City, Utah. Charlotte specializes in E-sports, Road Cycling, and Gravel, and holds certifications in USAC and Training Peaks coaching, along with expertise in sports nutrition and a B.A. in Psychology. Tune in as we explore Charlotte's inspiring journey and uncover valuable insights into cycling training, bikepacking, training indoors, and the intersection of nutrition and pscyhology.

item.title: Test Don't Guess? and What happens if you leave Zone 2?
item.itunes:title: Test Don't Guess? and What happens if you leave Zone 2?
item.description:
Join us in this episode as we tackle two thought-provoking listener questions. First up: the myth that you must undergo lab or physiological testing to effectively train. We break down the various testing options and offer insights into what to consider if you're contemplating physiological testing. Next, we delve into the topic of rigid zone-2 or endurance training—exploring whether leaving the zone momentarily jeopardizes the benefits.
 
This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs
 
This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. item.title: Libido, Training Couples & Racing After Kids - Megan + David Roche
item.itunes:title: Libido, Training Couples & Racing After Kids - Megan + David Roche
item.description:
Tune in to the latest episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, featuring Megan and David Roche from SWAP Running and 'The Happy Runner' fame! Bursting with positive energy, they join us for a lively discussion on running with training partners, managing run training with a child, coaching couples, racing together, and even delving into the topic of libido—just in time for Valentine's Day! Get ready for an episode packed with insights, laughs, and plenty of inspiration to fuel your athletic journey.

This Episode is brought to you by AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs

item.description:
Update: We recorded this episode again to bring the audio quality up to our standards. Thanks for checking it out again!

Join us as Shannon Vandivier, the Director and Executive Producer at Cold Collaborative, tells stories about the making of the series. item.title: A life of Adventure & The 7 Summits - Remy Kloos
item.itunes:title: A life of Adventure & The 7 Summits - Remy Kloos
item.description:
In this episode of the Consummate Athlete Podcast, we explore the remarkable life of Remy Kloos – a high-altitude mountaineer, mindset coach, and humanitarian hailing from South Africa. Our conversation delves into Remy's accomplishment of conquering the Seven Summits, scaling the highest peaks on each continent, including Mount Everest. We cover various topics, including preparation for big adventures, coping with altitude, and training for mountaineering. Remy shares insights on sustainability and recounts her personal journey from overcoming depression to discovering inner strength through mountain challenges.

This Episode is brought to you by Consummate Athlete Event and Goal Based Training Plans, available in the Training Peaks Store. item.title: Dr. Stacy Sims - Weight Lifting, Protein, Menopause [Rebroadcast]
item.itunes:title: Dr. Stacy Sims - Weight Lifting, Protein, Menopause [Rebroadcast]
item.description:
This is our most popular episode in 2023 so we are rebroadcasting this important interview with Dr. Stacy Sims. Her mission is to help women better understand their bodies and elevate them in research, science, sport, and ultimately, in life.  The ideas that that women can and should lift heavy and consume more protein are two that she has advanced in both research and media.

Check out books and research by Dr. Stacy Sims – https://www.drstacysims.com/

item.title: The Consummate Athlete 2024 Goal Setting Episode
item.itunes:title: The Consummate Athlete 2024 Goal Setting Episode
item.description:
Peter Glassford and Molly Hurford discuss goal setting for the next cycling or running season. They discuss Molly's annual New Year article with Canadian Cycling Magazine, how they are thinking about goal setting for themselves this year. A review of SMART goals and Outcome vs. Process/Practice goals. They also discuss letting go of goals that aren't serving you or have just been 'hanging around' for a few years!

item.content:encoded:
Peter Glassford and Molly Hurford discuss goal setting for the next cycling or running season. They discuss Molly's annual New Year article with Canadian Cycling Magazine, how they are thinking about goal setting for themselves this year. A review of SMART goals and Outcome vs. Process/Practice goals. They also discuss letting go of goals that aren't serving you or have just been 'hanging around' for a few years!

item.title: The Joy of Well Being with Colleen and Jason Wachob
item.itunes:title: The Joy of Well Being with Colleen and Jason Wachob
item.description:
In this episode, host Molly Hurford is joined by special guests Colleen and Jason Wachob, the duo behind the amazing website, mindbodygreen.com. 

Colleen and Jason share their wealth of knowledge and experiences, drawing from their latest venture—their new book, "The Joy of Wellbeing." Tune in to learn how well-being plays a crucial role in athletic performance and how incorporating these principles into your routine can lead to a happier and healthier lifestyle. Whether you're an elite athlete or someone looking to optimize their everyday well-being, this episode is packed with valuable insights and actionable advice.

item.title: How to Stay Motivated on the Indoor Trainer
item.itunes:title: How to Stay Motivated on the Indoor Trainer
item.description:
We discuss a question about staying motivated on the indoor cycling trainer .  This time of year can be hard to get going with the trainer and there are some people who are already getting sick of riding indoors if they started early! We discuss tactics to mix up your routine and keep yourself training through long winters.

item.title: Peaking for Races + Off-season Strength Only Blocks For Busy Cyclists
item.itunes:title: Peaking for Races + Off-season Strength Only Blocks For Busy Cyclists
item.description:
In this episode, we discuss two listener questions. First, we address the viability and wisdom of dedicating a significant portion of your training year, potentially a full month or more, to prioritize strength training while dialing back 'cardio' workouts like cycling or running. Next, we turn our attention to event peaking strategies for busy adult cyclists.

item.title: Off-Season Nutrition - Caroline Burkholder
item.itunes:title: Off-Season Nutrition - Caroline Burkholder
item.description:
Caroline Burkholder is a Registered Dietitian Nutritionist and Certified Eating Disorder Specialist based in Knoxville. She returns for her second episode on the Consummate Athlete to discuss intuitive eating, off-season nutrition for endurance athletes including the concepts of race weight, periodization, masters vs. pros and dealing with the holidays.

item.title: Bouncing Back from a DNF or a Challenging Season
item.itunes:title: Bouncing Back from a DNF or a Challenging Season
item.description:
In this episode, we tackle a listener's question about the process of learning from and moving forward after a challenging season ending with a DNF (Did Not Finish). We delve into the unpredictable nature of sports and competition, exploring the valuable lessons we can draw from both our victories and setbacks. 

Get Your Free Bonus from AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH – to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs

Download

Find links to your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://pod.link/1100471297

Show Notes

  • Post on similar topic - 'Use Failure to your Advantage' 
  • Race day mistakes we have made - https://consummateathlete.com/race-day-mistakes-that-weve-made/
  • molly and Peter fail on FKT attempt - https://consummateathlete.com/training-happy-dealing-with-injury-and-failure/
  • Limiters and ways to get faster - https://consummateathlete.com/limiters-ways-to-get-faster/

item.title: The Midlife Cyclist - Phil Cavell
item.itunes:title: The Midlife Cyclist - Phil Cavell
item.description:
Today we have an interview with Phil Cavell, the founder and CEO of cyclefit a centre in the U.K. devoted to modern bike-fitting.  We discuss his book 'The Midlife Cyclist'  and we go deep on a few bike-fitting concepts including foot-beds and feet!  We also spend lots of time on the challenges that middle age athletes face including muscle loss, heart conditions, pain, soreness and the downsides of indoor training.

This episode is supported  by 'The Strong Girl' The new book from Molly Hurford

Download

Find links to your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://pod.link/1100471297

Watch the Episodes on Youtube https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xsCGr1xTmH8

  • CYCLEFIT BIKE FITTING STUDIOS

item.title: Cross-Training is Not a Crime
item.itunes:title: Cross-Training is Not a Crime
item.description:
We made the t-shirt and we discuss the confusingly taboo topic of Cross-training for cyclists and runners today. Not only is cross-training ok but its important to your long term success and happiness!

item.title: Evolving Purpose in Sport - Viviane Favery
item.itunes:title: Evolving Purpose in Sport - Viviane Favery
item.description:
We met Viviane Favery at Quebec Singletrack Experience in 2023 and we were blown away by her communication skills (in multiple languages!). She is a MTB Skills Coach at Empire 47, has commentated World Cup Mountain Biking and hosts the MTB Pass Podcast. She also has a vast cycling experience ranging form Race Across America, World Cup Mountain Bike events and MTB Stage races. 

Follow Viviane on Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/vivianefavery/

This episode is brought to you by Phone Consultation with Consummate Athlete

Book a Consultation to review your season and your future goals

item.title: Fall Racing and Training when motivated and when you are not!
item.itunes:title: Fall Racing and Training when motivated and when you are not!
item.description:
Join us on this episode as we discuss two scenerios for the fall racing and training season. What do you do if you have lots of motivation, energy and health but no plans for racing? OR what to do if you have lots of races but can feel your motivation, energy or health slipping before the fall races ramp up?

item.title: Juliet & Kelly Starrett - Built to Move - 10 Vital Signs
item.itunes:title: Juliet & Kelly Starrett - Built to Move - 10 Vital Signs
item.description:
In this episode as we discuss 10 Vital Signs for tracking and enhancing your movement and health, as outlined in Kelly and Juliet Starrett's latest book, 'Built to Move'. These vital signs cover a wide spectrum, from getting up from the floor to measuring your sleep hours, and from tracking veggie/fruit consumption to assessing your shoulder's range of motion. Learn how these quick and simple assessments can be integrated into your daily routine for lasting health improvements. Juliet Starrett is a former whitewater rafting world champion and philanthropist who co-founded The Ready State (formerly Mobility Wod) alongside her husband, Kelly Starrett DPT.

Download

Find links to your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://pod.link/1100471297

Show Notes

  • Share this episode on Instagram or Facebook and tag @consummateathlete and @thereadystate before Sept 18, 2023 and be entered to win a copy of "Built to Move" or a subscription to the Ready State App. 
  • Subscribe to Consummate Athlete on Youtube to watch the latest episodes
  • Get your copy of "Build to Move" by Kelly and Juliet Starrett  https://amzn.to/3Osqybk
  • Sign up for the 21 day built to move challenge to help integrate the concepts into your life
  • Check out the ReadyState App for daily mobility videos, classes and specific exercises for injury in the palm of your hand!

item.title: Genetic Potential & Improving as You Age
item.itunes:title: Genetic Potential & Improving as You Age
item.description:

item.title: How to Change Disciplines - Ashton Lambie
item.itunes:title: How to Change Disciplines - Ashton Lambie
item.description:
Ashton Lambie is a professional cyclist and World Champion in Track cycling. He set the 4000m Individual Purusit record becoming the first person to break the 4-minute barrier. In the following years he has raced Gravel and completed long events and now is transitioning to sailng in the America's Cup! This pivoting is inspiring for Consummate Athletes looking to try new things, go new places and meet new people!

This episode is brought to you by GOODR Sunglasses. Goodr offers a 30 Day Money Back Guarantee and 100% Satisfaction. Find your pair at GOODR.com and use code consummate to get FREE shipping

item.title: How Intense Do You Need to Be & What to Do in Race Week
item.itunes:title: How Intense Do You Need to Be & What to Do in Race Week
item.description:
Today we discuss How intense you need to be. This question was sparked by a listener who has  gotten really into David Goggins and is curious how we think about pushing to 110% (or cranking it to 11). We also discuss race week, the overarching goals of the week or two before your big race and the nitty gritty of what to do for training that week. 

Get Your Free Bonus from AG1- drinkAG1.com/MOLLYH - to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs

 

Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete  

 

Show Notes

  • David Goggins book
  • Our Tenants (Peter's coaching philosophy) https://consummateathlete.com/3-tools-for-success-the-consummate-athlete-coaching-philosophy/
  • Seiler 'Myth of no pain, no gain' https://consummateathlete.com/seiler2020/

item.content:encoded:
Today we discuss How intense you need to be. This question was sparked by a listener who has gotten really into David Goggins and is curious how we think about pushing to 110% (or cranking it to 11). We also discuss race week, the overarching goals of the week or two before your big race and the nitty gritty of what to do for training that week.

item.title: Should You Increase Your Training Hours (or Cadence?)
item.itunes:title: Should You Increase Your Training Hours (or Cadence?)
item.description:
We tackle the common question of increasing training hours. In simple terms, more training time will increase fitness BUT when is more work just more work. We also discuss a question about cadence being too high. 

Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
This Episode is brought to you by GOODR Sunglasses - goodr offers a 30 Day Money Back Guarantee and 100% Satisfaction. Find your pair at goodr.com/consummate and use code consummate to get FREE shipping.
 
 
Show Notes
  • Flat pedals vs. Road Pedals vs. MTB Clipless
  • Recent Post on Saving time at home to add time to training
  • Post - On Risk and when more is just more

item.title: Can you Breathe Better? Luke Way of Isocapnic
item.itunes:title: Can you Breathe Better? Luke Way of Isocapnic
item.description:
Luke Way, an endurance coach and the founder of Isocapnic, joins us to talk about respiratory training and their tool the 'Breathe Way Better', which helps train the respiratory muscles separately from movement. We discuss what it is, how to do it, common questions and challenges with Respiratory Training. 

This episode is brought to you InsideTracker - For a limited time, you get 20% off the entire InsideTracker store when you sign up at insidetracker.com/consummate  (can also use CODE Consummate)

item.title: Racing Sick & Race if You are Going to Race
item.itunes:title: Racing Sick & Race if You are Going to Race
item.description:
We discuss the difficult decision to race if you are sick and how this relates to our 'race if you are going to race' concept of committing to race. A follow up on quick strength to avoid soreness and when doing a lot of endurance racing is also included. 

This Episode is sponsored by AG1 by Athletic Greens: 
 To make it easy, Athletic Greens is going to give you a FREE 1 year supply of immune-supporting Vitamin D AND 5 FREE travel packs with your first purchase. All you have to do is visit athleticgreens.com/mollyh to take ownership over your health and pick up the ultimate daily nutritional insurance!

item.title: What to do with Wearables and Data - Sian Allen
item.itunes:title: What to do with Wearables and Data - Sian Allen
item.description:
Dr. Sian Allen (DrSianAllen on Twitter) is a Human Performance Researcher with a demonstrated history of working in agile & high-pressure industry R&D and elite sport environments. She wore an Oura ring & a Whoop band on the same hand/wrist for ~12 months and distilled the results on her twitter account. We talk about this experience and much more in this episode. 
 
 
 
This episode is brought to you InsideTracker - For a limited time, you get 20% off the entire InsideTracker store when you sign up at insidetracker.com/consummate  (can also use CODE Consummate)

Dr. Sian Allen (DrSianAllen on Twitter) is a Human Performance Researcher with a demonstrated history of working in agile & high-pressure industry R&D and elite sport environments. She wore an Oura ring & a Whoop band on the same hand/wrist for ~12 months and distilled the results on her twitter account. We talk about this experience and much more in this episode. This episode is brought to you InsideTracker - For a limited time, you get 20% off the entire InsideTracker store when you sign up at insidetracker.com/consummate (can also use CODE Consummate) Show Notes

item.title: Running Injuries and Strength with Chris Johnson the PT
item.itunes:title: Running Injuries and Strength with Chris Johnson the PT
item.description:
Chris Johnson is a physical therapist based in Seattle, WA. With over twenty-plus years of experience as a clinician-coach and multi-sport athlete. Check out his instagram and youtube for great exercise videos and endurance content @chrisjohnsonthept. Today we discuss common running injuries and strength for busy endurance athletes. 

 

This episode is brought to you by our Training Peaks Training Plans for cyclists (link) - Use Code " CA25 " to get a discount on any of our plans - 
 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete

item.title: Bike Pack Adventures - Chris Panasky
item.itunes:title: Bike Pack Adventures - Chris Panasky
item.description:
Chris Panasky runs BikePackAdventures a podcast and website that offers information about bike packing in Canada. We discuss bike packing gear, tactics and get into the intricacies of 'bike pack racing', FKT and independant time trials (ITT) . The second half Chris interviews Peter about training for bike packing and how strength, intensity and shorter races might fit into the training plan. Make sure to check out his great map of routes and the Canadian Shield Bikepacking Summit in June 2023.

item.title: TSS what is it good for ? (absolutely ... )
item.itunes:title: TSS what is it good for ? (absolutely ... )
item.description:
We discuss what Training Stress Score (TSS ) and the associated metrics, numbers and system can be used for and several of the limitations, common errors and common questions . TLDR = when in doubt have fun and think about what you are trying to get better at rather than a single number that may not capture what matters for your sport, performance, long-term health/performance or enjoyment! 

Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
https://athleticgreens.com/mollyh" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Get Your Free Bonus from Athletic Greens - athleticgreens.com/mollyh - to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs
 
SHOW NOTES
  • BOOK A CALL with Coach Peter Glassford to discuss TSS
  • What is Fatigue Fitness and Form in Training Peaks on Consummate Athlete Coaching Blog
  • One Metric to Rule them all ( DW's Pants ' ) - Cautions on putting everything on one metric (like TSS) 
  • How High should my CTL be - post on consummate athlete blog

item.title: Choosing Your Races / Events For the Year
item.itunes:title: Choosing Your Races / Events For the Year
item.description:
Peter and Molly discuss how you can choose what races to do this year. Even if you are looking at big rides or events it is worth planning ahead for success. It can seem like there are many races and many weekends but once you eliminate ones that don't work well with family and work you may find that the answers are more obvious! 

Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
https://athleticgreens.com/mollyh" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Get Your Free Bonus from Athletic Greens - athleticgreens.com/mollyh - to receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs
 
 
Show Notes
  • Book a phone consult with Peter to discuss your Season Goals and Schedule
  • Bike Packing Conference in Ontario Canada via Chris Pansky 
  • Can you prepare for the goal you have set (post ) 
  • Can you set 'fun' goals (podcast) 
  • You can do it all but you can't do it all at once ! (post)\
  •

item.title: Basics of Testing for Cycling at Home
item.itunes:title: Basics of Testing for Cycling at Home
item.description:
Molly and Peter discuss ways to test at home for cycling (a.k.a. field or performance testing). They touch on indoor, outdoor methods and how to test if you don't have a power meter. They also go into common issues with testing (E.g. what is not a test) and why testing can be a great training intervention. 

This Episode is Sponsored by AG1 by Athletic Greens

item.title: Reaching Goals in 2023
item.itunes:title: Reaching Goals in 2023
item.description:
Molly and Peter discuss goal setting and a few different ways to think about goals ranging from setting 'intentions' to more formal or specific methods. We discuss Dan John's 5 x 2 frame work and how it might apply to an endurance athletes season and typical training blocks. Considerations around work and family goals and responsibilities are  discussed. 
 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
This episode is brought to you by Phone Consultations with Consummate Athlete  
Book a phone consultation to review your goals, your training plan or answer questions about your health, fitness or injuries

item.title: Finding Fun in Racing - Lea Davison
item.itunes:title: Finding Fun in Racing - Lea Davison
item.description:
Lea Davison is a 2x Olympic mountain biker from Vermont. She co-founded Little Bellas MTB an organization that gets girls on bikes and is an active advocate for LGBTQ rights. 

Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete

item.title: How Gravel Gave Amputee Sydney Marshburn Her Confidence Back
item.itunes:title: How Gravel Gave Amputee Sydney Marshburn Her Confidence Back
item.description:
Sydney Marshburn was a competitive swimmer until a condiiton required amputation of her left leg above the knee in August of 2021. As she recovered she entered and won an entry for the SBT GRVL race in a contest from Click Medical. Her story is inspiring!

 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete

item.description:
Kylee Van Horn returns to the Podcast to answer Training and Racing Nutrition Questions. Topics include overcoming 'trouble' eating during races, inability to swallow while racing, beet root juice, how much carb to take in, post - workout fueling, stage races and race foods.

item.title: Intervals on Trail? And how easy is too easy for endurance?
item.itunes:title: Intervals on Trail? And how easy is too easy for endurance?
item.description:
We discuss two listener questions today. 

1) Should you do intervals on the trail or on the road/track? 

2) How easy is too easy for endurance and how to set endurance zones. 

SPONSOR

7X World Champ. MTB & Gravel Hall of Famer. Author of Rusch To Glory. Emmy award winner for Blood Road. Founder of Rebecca's Private Idaho… Rebecca Rusch joined us to talk about all things gravel racing, career longevity, how to decide on your next goal and so much more.

item.title: Your August Bucket List Race Pep Talk
item.itunes:title: Your August Bucket List Race Pep Talk
item.description:
Breck Epic, Quebec Singletrack Experience, Leadville 100, Swiss Epic and our local favorite the 'Eager Beaver' are among the big August endurance races happening this coming weekend (usually the second weekend in August). We talk about this last week before the big races, enjoying the work you have done and the journey you are on and specific items from the technical guides we noted for each race. 

 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
This episode is brought to you by 3 Month 100% Made for You Training Plans by Consummate athlete
- These popular plans are made from scratch for you, your goals, your schedule, your gear, and your goals.
https://consummateathlete.com/training-plans/

item.title: When You Should Change Race Categories or Distance
item.itunes:title: When You Should Change Race Categories or Distance
item.description:
A common question we get is about upgrading categories or 'cat-ing up' and about going longer in endurance events. We discuss pros/cons and consisterations you might make before going to a more challenging or longer race. 

 
 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
This episode is brought to you by 3 Month 100% Made for You Training Plans by Consummate athlete

https://consummateathlete.com/training-plans/
 
Show Notes
  • Race if you are going to race (podcast)
  • Can you prepare for the goal you have set, and do you want to (post)

item.title: Racing Multiple Cycling Disciplines - Maghalie Rochette
item.itunes:title: Racing Multiple Cycling Disciplines - Maghalie Rochette
item.description:
Maghalie Rochette is a Professional Cyclist. She has won a Cyclocross World Cup and is a fan favorite due to her incredible personality and fun projects like CX Fever and the Faces of Cyclocross (to name a few). She talk to Molly about racing multiple seasons and getting sick this past summer with Covid.

item.title: Race Day Mistakes (we have made)
item.itunes:title: Race Day Mistakes (we have made)
item.description:
Fresh off of 2022 Canadian MTB Nationals and Molly's Quebec Mega Trails Running race we talk  about mistakes we made this year, in past years and some common themes we observed at the races and with clients. 

Our book Becoming a Consummate Athlete covers many of these 'little things' that pop up around organization, planning, mindset, fueling and remembering to enjoy success

 
 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete

item.itunes:title: Recovery from Big Races - Ellie Pell
item.description:
Ellie Pell is a Professional Trail Runner. She recently completed the Western States 100 miler in California and in this episode discusses running long and recovery from long races, especially high level or 'bucket list' races with a large physical and psychological buildup. 
 
This episode is brought to you by 3 Month 100% Made for You Training Plans by Consummate athlete
- These popular plans are made from scratch for you, your goals, your schedule, your gear, and your goals.

item.title: HRV Biofeedback and Deep Breathing - Marco Altini, PhD
item.itunes:title: HRV Biofeedback and Deep Breathing - Marco Altini, PhD
item.description:
"Heart Rate Variability (HRV) Biofeedback is a technique that can directly affect physiological and psychological factors through deep breathing exercises and is an ideal strategy to help us self-regulate and better cope with stressful situations​ " (HRV4BIOFEEDBACK APP).  Marco Altini, PhD is the creator of HRV4Training, which is used by many athletes to record and track their HRV each morning. Today we discuss the value of HRV Biofeedback to guide deep breathing. We discuss the value of deep breathing generally and how you can perform these exercises using a simple timer or stop watch, as well as by using the HRV biofeedback App - https://www.hrv4biofeedback.com/the-app.html 
 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete
 
This episode is brought to you by Ag1 by Athletic Greens
Get Your Free Bonus from Athletic Greens by using our link athleticgreens.com/mollyh - receive your bonus of a free 1-year supply of Vitamin D and 5 Free Travel Packs!
 
Show Notes
  • HRV biofeedback App - https://www.hrv4biofeedback.com/the-app.html 
  • Past episodes of the Consummate Athlete with Marco Altini
  • Marco's article in MEdium on 30 days of HRV guided Biofeedback

item.title: Wearing Headphones While Training?
item.itunes:title: Wearing Headphones While Training?

item.title: 5 Things To Help with Hot, Long Races - Western States Race
item.itunes:title: 5 Things To Help with Hot, Long Races - Western States Race
item.description:
Molly shares her experience pacing Karen Holland at the 2022 Western States 100 mile running race. This is one of the most competitive 100 milers in the world and unsurprisingly is a grueling course due to the heat, elevation and course.

https://consummateathlete.com/training-plans/
 
Show Notes
  • Heat Training Tips Post

  • Western States Running Race

item.title: The Laws of Cycling - Ian Brisbin
item.itunes:title: The Laws of Cycling - Ian Brisbin
item.description:
Ian Brisbin is an avid cyclist and this passion manifests itself in his work, particularly through his focus upon the law as it relates to people on bikes, and cyclists injured by drivers in crashes. He engages in cycling and active transportation public interest advocacy, and uses his law practice at Martin & Hillyer to amplify the voices of seriously injured cyclists through www.velolaw.ca.

item.title: Is It Normal to Struggle with Returning to Racing?

item.itunes:title: Running on Veggies - Lottie Bildirici
item.description:
Lottie Bildirici is an athlete, blogger, recipe developer, and Holistic Health Coach. Her website and blog, Running on Veggies, share delicious recipes and tips for what and how to eat to reach peak physical performance. Today We discuss 'healthy' vs. 'unhealthy foods' and plant-based diets for athletes.

item.title: Power To Weight
item.itunes:title: Power To Weight
item.description:
Today we discuss a question about the effect of added weight on climbing speed and more globally about the Power:Weight ratio. Bodyweight can be a difficult topic and we hope that discussing it today will help move the balance towards focusing on enjoying sport and a focus on improving our skills and (at times) our power/performance.
 
Download or find links in your favorite Podcast App (remember to rate and review!) https://directory.libsyn.com/shows/view/id/consummateathlete 
 
This episode is brought to you by 3 Month 100% Made for You Training Plans by Consummate athlete
- These popular plans are made from scratch for you, your goals, your schedule, your gear, and your goals.
 https://consummateathlete.com/training-plans/
 
 
Show Notes
  • Happy Runner Episode w. David and Megan Roche
  • Happy Runner Book Link
  • XCO MTB Study on determinants of performance (highly variable, importance of start)  + HERE  + HERE on pacing
  • Fatigue/performance in Ultra Running Study - race experience and cognitive factors included 
  • Rehrer et al. (1992) analysed the gastrointestinal (GI) distress in well-trained athletes participating in the Swiss Alpine Marathon (67 km). The authors reported a GI distress in 42% of men and 57% of women.
  • Completion of races as source of improved mental toughness/dedication to sport in  ultra-runners
  • CTS article https://trainright.com/power-weight-ratio-importance-for-cycling/

item.title: Outlaw 100 Mile Trail Race Debrief w. Molly Hurford
item.itunes:title: Outlaw 100 Mile Trail Race Debrief w. Molly Hurford
item.description:
Molly tells us about her first 100 mile Trail Run Race! It was the Outlaw 100 Mile Trail Race in Oklahoma and she won, setting the overall course record! We discuss what she would have done differently, what went well, what she ate, how she paced, and more!

item.description:

item.title: Fueling Your Training, Body Image, Overtraining - Alex Coates
item.itunes:title: Fueling Your Training, Avoiding Overtraining - Alex Coates
item.description:
Alexandra Coates is a retired ITU elite triathlete, a triathlon coach, and a Ph.D. candidate in exercise physiology. She returns to compare Overtraining and Underfueling. We also discuss body-image vs. athletic goals and how adequate carbohydrates might interact with energy availability. 

This episode is brought to you by 3 Month 100% Made for You Training Plans - These popular plans are made from scratch for you, your goals, your schedule, your gear, and your goals.
